seoTitle: Import Stock Tax Information Into TurboTax 2026
metaDescription: Learn how to import stock tax information into TurboTax from your brokerage account in 2026, plus tips for accurate capital gains reporting.
intro: Manually entering every stock trade is tedious and error-prone, which is why importing directly from your brokerage is worth setting up correctly. This guide covers how to import stock tax information into TurboTax for 2026, connecting your brokerage account to pull in capital gains and losses automatically. It's useful for active traders, small business owners, and finance professionals managing tax filings for multiple clients.

steps.heading: Get an overview of TurboTax's import features
steps.body: See how TurboTax connects with brokerage accounts to bring in your stock trading data directly.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step importing process
steps.body: Walk through connecting your brokerage account and importing your stock tax information into TurboTax.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for accuracy
steps.body: Review your imported data against your brokerage statements to reduce the risk of errors before filing.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common issues
steps.body: If the import doesn't go as expected, common problems and their fixes are covered so you're not stuck manually entering everything.

faq.question: Which brokerage accounts can I import stock data from into TurboTax?
faq.answer: TurboTax is shown working with different brokerage accounts to pull in trading data, though exact compatibility depends on your specific brokerage.

faq.question: Why should I import instead of manually entering stock trades?
faq.answer: Importing reduces manual entry and the risk of errors, which matters most for active traders with a high volume of transactions.

faq.question: What if my stock data doesn't import correctly?
faq.answer: The video covers common import issues and troubleshooting steps to help resolve problems when your brokerage data doesn't come through cleanly.

faq.question: Is this useful for small business owners, not just individual traders?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial notes benefits for small business owners and independent contractors who also need streamlined capital gains and losses reporting.
